title: Power module based on normally-on semiconductor switches
abstract: A power module (10) comprises a plurality of normally-on semiconductor switches (14) based on a wide bandgap substrate, the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) connected in parallel; and a balancing unit (24) comprising a capacitor (32) and a balancing semiconductor switch (34) connected in series, which are connected in parallel to the normally-on semiconductor switches (14).
claims: Claims 1. A power module (10), comprising: a plurality of normally-on semiconductor switches (14) based on a wide bandgap substrate, the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) connected in parallel; a balancing unit (24) comprising a capacitor (32) and a balancing semiconductor switch (34) connected in series, which are connected in parallel to the normally-on semiconductor switches (14); wherein no inductor is connected in parallel with the plurality of normally-on semiconductor switches (14) and with the capacitor (32) and the balancing semiconductor switch (34) connected in series. 2. The power module (10) of claim 1, wherein solely the plurality of normally-on semiconductor switches (14) and the capacitor (32) and the balancing semiconductor switch (34) connected in series are connected in parallel. 3. The power module (10) of claim 1 or 2, further comprising: a controller (26) adapted for providing a gate signal for the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) and for providing a gate signal for the balancing semiconductor switch (34); wherein the controller (26) is adapted for turning the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) on and off with a given switching frequency; wherein the controller (26) is adapted for turning off the balancing semiconductor switch (34), when the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are turned on and for turning on the balancing semiconductor switch (34), when the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are turned off. 4. The power module (10) of claim 3, wherein the given switching frequency is higher than 50 Hz; wherein a time interval between turning off the balancing semiconductor switch (34) and turning on the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) is smaller than ÎÎ¼Î²; wherein a time interval between turning off the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) and turning on the balancing semiconductor switch (34) is smaller than ÎÎ¼Î². 5. The power module (10) of claim 4 or 5, wherein the controller (26) is mechanically integrated in the power module (10). 6. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, wherein the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are based on a SiC substrate; wherein the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are JFETs. 7. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, wherein the balancing semiconductor switch (34) is a normally-off semiconductor switch. 8. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, wherein the balancing semiconductor switch (34) is based on a Si substrate; wherein the balancing semiconductor switch (34) is an IGBT, a MOSFET or a JFET. 9. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, wherein the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) comprise gates (20), all of which gates (20) are electrically connected with each other for receiving a single gate signal from a controller (26). 10. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, further comprising: a base plate (68) to which the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are bonded and a top plate (66) to which for each normally-on semiconductor switch (14) an electrically conducting spring element (70) is attached, which presses against the respective normally-on semiconductor switch (14). 11. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, further comprising: an auxiliary unit (48) for providing auxiliary power to the controller (26), wherein the auxiliary unit (48) comprises a battery (52) for providing power to the controller (26) in the case of a start-up of the power module (10). 12. The power module (10) of one of the previous claims, further comprising: an auxiliary unit (48) for providing auxiliary power to the controller (26), wherein the auxiliary unit (48) comprises an inductive power supply (60) for providing power to the controller (26) in the case of a start-up of the power module (10). 13. A power module stack (38), comprising: at least two power modules (10) according to one of the previous claims connected in series. 14. A use of a power module (10) according to one of the claims 1 to 12 for switching a current in a converter. 15. A method for controlling a power module (10) according to one of the claims 1 to 12, the method comprising: turning the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) on and off with a given switching frequency; turning off the balancing semiconductor switch (34), when the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are turned on and turning on the balancing semiconductor switch (34), when the normally-on semiconductor switches (14) are turned off.
